description_long: This object is a floatplane with a high-mounted straight wing and a single engine mounted at the nose, featuring a prominent propeller. The fuselage is streamlined and painted primarily gray with blue accents on the engine cowling, wing tips, and tail. The aircraft is equipped with two large floats beneath the fuselage for water landings, supported by struts connecting to the wings and fuselage. The tail section has a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with windows visible around the nose area. The overall design suggests a utility or bush plane suitable for both urban and remote environments with water access.
description: A gray and blue floatplane with a high-mounted straight wing, single nose-mounted propeller engine, and large twin floats for water landings. It features a conventional tail and an enclosed forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the high-mounted wing, single propeller engine at the nose, and the twin floats beneath the fuselage.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide wingspan, twin floats, and the propeller at the nose, with the cockpit windows visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same high-wing and float configuration, with clear visibility of the tail and engine details.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail assembly, the full span of the high-mounted wing, and the alignment of the floats beneath the fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Twin float landing gear with fixed struts.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with visible windows, likely single or dual seat.
